macro_markets.query: What are the most important macroeconomic and stock market news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major index movements, Fed/central bank news, and significant company earnings or announcements.

items.title: S&P 500 Falls 1.01% as Chip Selloff Deepens Amid AI Valuation Concerns
items.summary: The S&P 500 closed down 1.01% on Friday, led by a steep decline in chip stocks that marked their worst weekly performance in over a year, sparking doubts about the sustainability of the AI-driven rally [1][2].

items.title: Fed Policymakers Swell in Support of Rate Hike as Inflation Persists
items.summary: Cleveland Fed President Beth Hammack joined a growing chorus of officials arguing interest rates may need to rise to combat persistent inflation, setting up a contentious debate at the July meeting under Chairman Kevin Warsh [1].

items.title: Oil Surges to $85 as US-Iran Tensions Cloud Strait of Hormuz Outlook
items.summary: Brent crude nears $85 a barrel and WTI jumped 3.50% amid renewed hostilities between Washington and Tehran, with Trump announcing a cargo fee for ships passing the Strait of Hormuz [2][3].

items.title: Apple Overtakes Nvidia to Regain World's Most Valuable Company Spot
items.summary: Apple regained the top global valuation at $4.88 trillion after Nvidia's market value dropped 3.5%, reversing their earlier swap [3].

items.title: Netflix Shares Plunge After Weak Forecast Adds to Market Pressure
items.summary: Netflix stock fell sharply after issuing a weak forecast, contributing to Friday's selloff in US equity futures alongside the chip downturn [1][6].

items.title: US Imposes 25% Tariffs on Some Brazilian Imports Over Unfair Trade Practices
items.summary: The US announced new 25% tariffs on select Brazilian goods including after a yearlong investigation concluded Brazil engaged in unfair trade practices, though beef, coffee, and rare-earths were exempted [3][5].

items.title: China Keeps Benchmark Lending Rates Unchanged for 14th Month Despite Slowing Growth
items.summary: China is expected to hold benchmark lending rates steady in July despite softer-than-expected Q2 economic data highlighting uneven growth in its economy [1].

items.title: IBM Shares Drop After Preliminary Q2 Sales Miss Expectations
items.summary: International Business Machines Corp. fell after reporting preliminary Q2 sales below expectations, attributing the miss to clients shifting spending toward chips and servers [6].

items.title: Asian Markets Drop on Chip Selloff and Concerns Over Chinese AI Advances
items.summary: Asian stocks declined sharply Friday as chipmakers dragged Wall Street lower and new technological advances by a Chinese AI company intensified fears about the AI spending spree [1][5].

items.title: U.S. Consumer Sentiment Hits Five-Month High Despite Middle East Conflict Risks
items.summary: U.S. consumer sentiment rose to a five-month high in July due to lower gas prices, though experts warn the improvement may be temporary if Middle East conflict intensifies and fuels prices rise again [1][5].

crypto_news.query: What are the most significant cryptocurrency and blockchain news from the past 24 hours? Include major price movements, regulatory news, and notable project developments.

items.title: Bitcoin Slides Near $63,900 Amid Broader Market Selloff and Negative Coinbase Premium
items.summary: Bitcoin traded near $63,930 on July 18, up 0.18% over 24 hours but sliding below $63,000 the previous day as a selloff in AI and semiconductor stocks spilled into crypto, with Coinbase premium staying negative for a record 60 days[1][2].

items.title: Uniswap Governance Votes on v4 Fees and Robinhood Chain Expansion, Driving UNI Token Up 3%
items.summary: The UNI token gained nearly 3% as Uniswap governance votes on new proposals to enable protocol fees across Ethereum, Robinhood Chain, Base, and Arbitrum, which would strengthen the existing UNI burn mechanism[1][3][8].

items.title: US Senate CLARITY Act Negotiations Stall as Democrats Reject Republican Proposal
items.summary: Regulatory uncertainty increased as Senate talks on the CLARITY Act, which aims to clarify SEC and CFTC authority over digital assets, slowed after Democrats rejected the latest Republican proposal, facing a four-week deadline[2][8].

items.title: FTX to Distribute $900 Million to Creditors in Fifth Wave of Payouts on July 31
items.summary: FTX confirmed it will distribute roughly $900 million to eligible creditors starting July 31, with payments expected within one to three business days, potentially impacting market liquidity[1][8].

items.title: Bitcoin and Ethereum Spot ETFs See Continued Net Inflows Despite Market Volatility
items.summary: Bitcoin spot ETFs recorded $132 million in net inflows for the fourth consecutive day, while Ethereum spot ETFs saw $36.7 million in inflows, led by BlackRock's ETHA with $31.7 million[7].

items.title: Whales Linked to a16z Sell $25.3 Million in Hyperliquid (HYPE) Tokens
items.summary: On-chain monitoring revealed whales suspected to be related to a16z sold 421,800 HYPE tokens valued at $25.3 million over the past 24 hours, contributing to a sharp price drop for the DeX token[7][9].

items.title: SBI Holdings Completes Majority Acquisition of Coinhako Following MAS Approval
items.summary: SBI Holdings finalized the majority acquisition of Singapore-based crypto platform Coinhako after receiving approval from the Monetary Authority of Singapore (MAS), marking a significant deal in the Asian crypto market[1].

items.title: Nigeria Establishes Virtual Asset Commission and Regulatory Framework via Executive Order
items.summary: Nigerian President Bola Tinubu signed an executive order establishing a regulatory framework for virtual assets and creating a virtual asset commission, with the CBN launching a regulatory sandbox to implement the order[4].

ai_news.query: What are the most important artificial intelligence and machine learning news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include new model releases, company announcements, research breakthroughs, and industry trends.

items.title: Moonshot AI Releases Kimi K3, World's Largest Open-Weight AI Model
items.summary: Chinese startup Moonshot AI unveiled Kimi K3, a 2.8-trillion-parameter open-weight model with a 1-million-token context window, claiming performance rivaling Anthropic's frontier models; full open-source weights will drop by July 27.

items.title: xAI's Grok 4.6 Nears Training Completion with 2-Trillion Parameters
items.summary: Elon Musk confirmed xAI's next flagship model, Grok 4.6, is a 2-trillion-parameter system (up from 1.5T in Grok 4.5) with initial training finishing next week and broader availability expected by August 2026.

items.title: NVIDIA Launches Cosmos 3 Edge and National Physical-AI Factory in Japan
items.summary: NVIDIA introduced Cosmos 3 Edge, a 4B-parameter on-device world model for robotics, and partnered with Japan to launch the world's first national physical-AI factory featuring 27,500 Rubin GPUs.

items.title: Anthropic Launches Claude for Teachers and Prepares for Fall IPO
items.summary: Anthropic released Claude for Teachers with free verified-educator access and agentic tools, while quietly staffing up for a fall IPO at a reported $965 billion valuation.

items.title: Databricks Secures $3B Investment at $188B Valuation
items.summary: Investment firm Coatue Management is leading a $3 billion investment in data analytics provider Databricks, valuing the company at $188 billion according to the Wall Street Journal.

items.title: Apple Intelligence Approved for Launch in China via Alibaba and Baidu
items.summary: China's Cyberspace Administration approved Apple Intelligence for iPhones in China after Apple integrated Alibaba's Qwen and Baidu models, lifting Alibaba shares 4% and unlocking a delayed market.

items.title: OpenAI Unveils GPT-Red for Automated Red-Teaming Against Prompt Injection
items.summary: OpenAI introduced GPT-Red, an internal automated red-teaming LLM that reduced prompt injection success rates from 90%+ on GPT-5 to under 23% on GPT-5.6, hitting 84% attack success vs 13% for human red-teamers.

items.title: National Protests Target AI Data Center Buildout Across 125 US Locations
items.summary: Opponents of rapid AI infrastructure expansion plan coordinated protests on Saturday in at least 125 US locations, marking the first national effort to channel anger at data center buildouts that have roiled local politics.

items.title: Apple Overtakes Nvidia as World's Most Valuable Company
items.summary: Apple surpassed Nvidia on Friday to become the world's most valuable company as investors reassess AI outlooks, reshuffling top tech heavyweight rankings.

items.title: Thinking Machines Lab Launches First Model 'Inkling'
items.summary: Thinking Machines Lab, co-founded by former OpenAI CTO Mira Murati, launched its first in-house AI model named Inkling.

gaming.query: What are the most notable video game and gaming industry news from the past 24 hours? Include game releases, announcements, esports, and industry developments.

items.title: Halo: Campaign Evolved Launches July 28 with Premium Upgrade Details
items.summary: Halo Studios announces Halo: Campaign Evolved will launch on July 28, 2026, revealing pricing and early access dates for the Premium Upgrade tier.

items.title: ZA/UM Lays Off One-Third of Staff Following Zero Parades Launch
items.summary: The creators of Disco Elysium are laying off up to 32 employees, roughly one-third of their workforce, just two months after releasing Zero Parades: For Dead Spies.

items.title: Battlefield 6 Season 4 Confirmed for July 21 with Naval Warfare Focus
items.summary: EA and Battlefield Studios confirm Season 4 arrives July 21 on all platforms, featuring a new gameplay deep dive into naval warfare ahead of the release.

items.title: Zenless Zone Zero Version 3.1 Update Set for July 29 to Mark Second Anniversary
items.summary: MiHoYo announces the Version 3.1 update for Zenless Zone Zero on July 29, 2026, introducing new agents, a permanent mode, and anniversary rewards across PC, PS5, mobile, and Xbox.

items.title: Gallipoli Tactical WWI Shooter Rescheduled for August 20 Launch
items.summary: Blackmill Games confirms a new release date for Gallipoli, its tactical World War I multiplayer shooter, now launching August 20 on PS5, Xbox Series X/S, and PC.

items.title: Call of Duty League Championship: Riyadh Falcons Defeat Los Angeles Thieves
items.summary: At the Call of Duty League Championship Weekend 2026 in Riyadh, the Riyadh Falcons delivered a surprising defeat to the Los Angeles Thieves.

items.title: Assassin's Creed Black Flag Resynced Surpasses 3 Million Copies Sold
items.summary: Ubisoft announces that the remastered Assassin's Creed Black Flag has already sold over 3 million copies since its release.

items.title: GTA VI Pre-Orders Reportedly Hit $260 Million in First Week
items.summary: A new report suggests Grand Theft Auto VI generated $260 million in pre-orders during its first week, with potential launch revenue reaching $5 billion.

tech_news.query: What are the most important technology news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major product launches, company news, and significant tech industry developments. Exclude AI-specific news.

items.title: Fox Corp. to Acquire Streaming Platform Roku in $22 Billion Deal
items.summary: Fox Corp. announced a cash-and-stock deal to buy streaming pioneer Roku, valued at approximately $22 billion, marking a major consolidation in the streaming industry.

items.title: Apple Becomes World's Most Valuable Company, Overtaking Nvidia
items.summary: Apple overtook Nvidia on Friday to become the world's most valuable company with a market capitalization approaching $5 trillion, reshuffling the top ranks of tech heavyweights.

items.title: TSMC Pledges Another $100 Billion to Expand US Chipmaking Capacity
items.summary: Taiwan computer chipmaker TSMC pledged an additional $100 billion investment to expand its semiconductor manufacturing capacity in the United States.

items.title: SpaceX Acquires AI Coding Startup Cursor for $60 Billion
items.summary: Elon Musk's SpaceX is moving forward with a $60 billion acquisition of AI coding startup Cursor to gain a competitive edge against rivals Anthropic and OpenAI.

items.title: New York Blocks New Large Data Centers for Up to One Year
items.summary: New York state will block the construction of new large data centers for up to a year to protect the environment and energy grid amid criticism of rapid AI infrastructure buildout.

items.title: Shanghai Xingshu Tiansuan Launches First Space-Computing Satellite Constellation
items.summary: Shanghai Xingshu Tiansuan Space Technology Co. launched the first constellation of a space-computing project that aims to deploy a total of 1,000 satellites.

items.title: US Data Center Protests Go National with 125 Locations
items.summary: Opponents of rapid data center buildout held coordinated national protests on Saturday in at least 125 locations across the US, channeling anger at AI infrastructure expansion.

items.title: Colombian Energy Company Ecopetrol Hit by Cyberattack Stealing 3,300 User Accounts
items.summary: State-controlled energy company Ecopetrol reported a cyberattack resulting in the theft of data tied to about 3,300 user accounts, with potential material adverse financial impact.

items.title: Judge Rejects Meta Employees' Bid to Block Layoffs Amid AI Discrimination Claims
items.summary: A US judge rejected a bid by 26 Meta employees to block layoffs while they pursue claims that they were targeted for job cuts by the company's AI-powered tools due to disabilities or medical leave.

items.title: Chip Stocks Face Steepest Weekly Decline in Over a Year
items.summary: A rotation out of recent rally winners sent chip stocks toward their steepest weekly decline in more than a year, sparking concerns about the sustainability of the market surge.
